<a name="TriggerLevelWriter"></a>
## type [TriggerLevelWriter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/writer.go#L237-L253>)

TriggerLevelWriter buffers log lines at the ConditionalLevel or below until a trigger level \(or higher\) line is emitted. Log lines with level higher than ConditionalLevel are always written out to the destination writer. If trigger never happens, buffered log lines are never written out.

It can be used to configure "log level per request".

```go
type TriggerLevelWriter struct {
    // Destination writer. If LevelWriter is provided (usually), its WriteLevel is used
    // instead of Write.
    io.Writer

    // ConditionalLevel is the level (and below) at which lines are buffered until
    // a trigger level (or higher) line is emitted. Usually this is set to DebugLevel.
    ConditionalLevel Level

    // TriggerLevel is the lowest level that triggers the sending of the conditional
    // level lines. Usually this is set to ErrorLevel.
    TriggerLevel Level
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="TriggerLevelWriter.Close"></a>
### func \(\*TriggerLevelWriter\) [Close](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/writer.go#L337>)

```go
func (w *TriggerLevelWriter) Close() error
```

Close closes the writer and returns the buffer to the pool.

<a name="TriggerLevelWriter.Trigger"></a>
### func \(\*TriggerLevelWriter\) [Trigger](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/writer.go#L329>)

```go
func (w *TriggerLevelWriter) Trigger() error
```

Trigger forces flushing the buffer and change the trigger state to triggered, if the writer has not already been triggered before.

<a name="TriggerLevelWriter.WriteLevel"></a>
### func \(\*TriggerLevelWriter\) [WriteLevel](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/writer.go#L255>)

```go
func (w *TriggerLevelWriter) WriteLevel(l Level, p []byte) (n int, err error)
```



# diode

```go
import "github.com/rs/zerolog/diode"
```

Package diode provides a thread\-safe, lock\-free, non\-blocking io.Writer wrapper.

## Index

- [type Alerter](<#Alerter>)
- [type Writer](<#Writer>)
  - [func NewWriter\(w io.Writer, size int, pollInterval time.Duration, f Alerter\) Writer](<#NewWriter>)
  - [func \(dw Writer\) Close\(\) error](<#Writer.Close>)
  - [func \(dw Writer\) Write\(p \[\]byte\) \(n int, err error\)](<#Writer.Write>)


<a name="Alerter"></a>
## type [Alerter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/diode.go#L20>)



```go
type Alerter func(missed int)
```

<a name="Writer"></a>
## type [Writer](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/diode.go#L29-L34>)

Writer is a io.Writer wrapper that uses a diode to make Write lock\-free, non\-blocking and thread safe.

```go
type Writer struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewWriter"></a>
### func [NewWriter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/diode.go#L51>)

```go
func NewWriter(w io.Writer, size int, pollInterval time.Duration, f Alerter) Writer
```

NewWriter creates a writer wrapping w with a many\-to\-one diode in order to never block log producers and drop events if the writer can't keep up with the flow of data.

Use a diode.Writer when

```
wr := diode.NewWriter(w, 1000, 0, func(missed int) {
    log.Printf("Dropped %d messages", missed)
})
log := zerolog.New(wr)
```

If pollInterval is greater than 0, a poller is used otherwise a waiter is used.

See code.cloudfoundry.org/go\-diodes for more info on diode.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/diode"
)

func main() {
	w := diode.NewWriter(os.Stdout, 1000, 0, func(missed int) {
		fmt.Printf("Dropped %d messages\n", missed)
	})
	log := zerolog.New(w)
	log.Print("test")

	w.Close()

}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"debug","message":"test"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Writer.Close"></a>
### func \(Writer\) [Close](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/diode.go#L84>)

```go
func (dw Writer) Close() error
```

Close releases the diode poller and call Close on the wrapped writer if io.Closer is implemented.

<a name="Writer.Write"></a>
### func \(Writer\) [Write](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/diode.go#L74>)

```go
func (dw Writer) Write(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```



# hlog

```go
import "github.com/rs/zerolog/hlog"
```

Package hlog provides a set of http.Handler helpers for zerolog.

<details><summary>Example (Handler)</summary>
<p>



```go
//go:build !binary_log
// +build !binary_log

package main

import (
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"time"

	"net/http/httptest"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/hlog"
)

// fake alice to avoid dep
type middleware func(http.Handler) http.Handler
type alice struct {
	m []middleware
}

func (a alice) Append(m middleware) alice {
	a.m = append(a.m, m)
	return a
}
func (a alice) Then(h http.Handler) http.Handler {
	for i := range a.m {
		h = a.m[len(a.m)-1-i](h)
	}
	return h
}

func init() {
	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2001, time.February, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, time.UTC)
	}
}

func main() {
	log := zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().
		Timestamp().
		Str("role", "my-service").
		Str("host", "local-hostname").
		Logger()

	c := alice{}

	// Install the logger handler with default output on the console
	c = c.Append(hlog.NewHandler(log))

	// Install some provided extra handlers to set some request's context fields.
	// Thanks to those handlers, all our logs will come with some pre-populated fields.
	c = c.Append(hlog.RemoteAddrHandler("ip"))
	c = c.Append(hlog.UserAgentHandler("user_agent"))
	c = c.Append(hlog.RefererHandler("referer"))
	//c = c.Append(hlog.RequestIDHandler("req_id", "Request-Id"))

	// Here is your final handler
	h := c.Then(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		// Get the logger from the request's context. You can safely assume it
		// will be always there: if the handler is removed, hlog.FromRequest
		// will return a no-op logger.
		hlog.FromRequest(r).Info().
			Str("user", "current user").
			Str("status", "ok").
			Msg("Something happened")
	}))
	http.Handle("/", h)

	h.ServeHTTP(httptest.NewRecorder(), &http.Request{})

}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"info","role":"my-service","host":"local-hostname","user":"current user","status":"ok","time":"2001-02-03T04:05:06Z","message":"Something happened"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="RequestHandler"></a>
## func [RequestHandler](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/hlog/hlog.go#L66>)

```go
func RequestHandler(fieldKey string) func(next http.Handler) http.Handler
```

<a name="RequestIDHandler"></a>
## func [RequestIDHandler](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/hlog/hlog.go#L214>)

```go
func RequestIDHandler(fieldKey, headerName string) func(next http.Handler) http.Handler
```

RequestIDHandler returns a handler setting a unique id to the request which can be gathered using IDFromRequest\(req\). This generated id is added as a field to the logger using the passed fieldKey as field name. The id is also added as a response header if the headerName is not empty.

The generated id is a URL safe base64 encoded mongo object\-id\-like unique id. Mongo unique id generation algorithm has been selected as a trade\-off between size and ease of use: UUID is less space efficient and snowflake requires machine configuration.


```go
var BooleanArrayTestCases = []struct {
    Val    []bool
    Binary string
    Json   string
}{

    {[]bool{false}, "\x81\xf4", "[false]"},
    {[]bool{true, false, true}, "\x83\xf5\xf4\xf5", "[true,false,true]"},
    {[]bool{true, false, false, true, false, true}, "\x86\xf5\xf4\xf4\xf5\xf4\xf5", "[true,false,false,true,false,true]"},
```

# journald

```go
import "github.com/rs/zerolog/journald"
```

## Index

- [func NewJournalDWriter\(\) io.Writer](<#NewJournalDWriter>)


<a name="NewJournalDWriter"></a>
## func [NewJournalDWriter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/journald/journald.go#L38>)

```go
func NewJournalDWriter() io.Writer
```

NewJournalDWriter returns a zerolog log destination to be used as parameter to New\(\) calls. Writing logs to this writer will send the log messages to journalD running in this system.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/journald"
)

func main() {
	log := zerolog.New(journald.NewJournalDWriter())
	log.Info().Str("foo", "bar").Uint64("small", 123).Float64("float", 3.14).Uint64("big", 1152921504606846976).Msg("Journal Test")
}
```

#### Output

```

```

</p>
</details>

# log

```go
import "github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
```

Package log provides a global logger for zerolog.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

This example uses command\-line flags to demonstrate various outputs depending on the chosen log level.

```go
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()
	debug := flag.Bool("debug", false, "sets log level to debug")

	flag.Parse()

	// Default level for this example is info, unless debug flag is present
	zerolog.SetGlobalLevel(zerolog.InfoLevel)
	if *debug {
		zerolog.SetGlobalLevel(zerolog.DebugLevel)
	}

	log.Debug().Msg("This message appears only when log level set to Debug")
	log.Info().Msg("This message appears when log level set to Debug or Info")

	if e := log.Debug(); e.Enabled() {
		// Compute log output only if enabled.
		value := "bar"
		e.Str("foo", value).Msg("some debug message")
	}

}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"info","time":1199811905,"message":"This message appears when log level set to Debug or Info"}
```

</p>
</details>

## Variables

<a name="Logger"></a>Logger is the global logger.

```go
var Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stderr).With().Timestamp().Logger()
```

<a name="Ctx"></a>
## func [Ctx](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L129>)

```go
func Ctx(ctx context.Context) *zerolog.Logger
```

Ctx returns the Logger associated with the ctx. If no logger is associated, a disabled logger is returned.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of using the Ctx function in the log package to log with context

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

type valueKeyType int

var valueKey valueKeyType = 42

var captainHook = zerolog.HookFunc(func(e *zerolog.Event, l zerolog.Level, msg string) {
	e.Interface("key", e.GetCtx().Value(valueKey))
	e.Bool("is_error", l > zerolog.ErrorLevel)
	e.Int("msg_len", len(msg))
})

func main() {
	setup()

	hooked := log.Hook(captainHook)
	ctx := context.WithValue(context.Background(), valueKey, "12345")
	logger := hooked.With().Ctx(ctx).Logger()
	log.Ctx(logger.WithContext(ctx)).Info().Msg("hello world")
}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"info","time":1199811905,"key":"12345","is_error":false,"msg_len":11,"message":"hello world"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Debug"></a>
## func [Debug](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L59>)

```go
func Debug() *zerolog.Event
```

Debug starts a new message with debug level.

You must call Msg on the returned event in order to send the event.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of a log at a particular "level" \(in this case, "debug"\)

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()
	log.Debug().Msg("hello world")

}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"debug","time":1199811905,"message":"hello world"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err"></a>
## func [Err](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L45>)

```go
func Err(err error) *zerolog.Event
```

Err starts a new message with error level with err as a field if not nil or with info level if err is nil.

You must call Msg on the returned event in order to send the event.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of a conditional level based on the presence of an error.

```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()
	err := errors.New("some error")
	log.Err(err).Msg("hello world")
	log.Err(nil).Msg("hello world")

}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"error","error":"some error","time":1199811905,"message":"hello world"}
{"level":"info","time":1199811905,"message":"hello world"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Error"></a>

```go
func Error() *zerolog.Event
```

Error starts a new message with error level.

You must call Msg on the returned event in order to send the event.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of a log at a particular "level" \(in this case, "error"\)

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems

func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}

}
```

<a name="Fatal"></a>
## func [Fatal](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L88>)

```go
func Fatal() *zerolog.Event
```

Fatal starts a new message with fatal level. The os.Exit\(1\) function is called by the Msg method.

You must call Msg on the returned event in order to send the event.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of a log at a particular "level" \(in this case, "fatal"\)

```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()
	err := errors.New("A repo man spends his life getting into tense situations")
	service := "myservice"

	log.Fatal().
		Err(err).
		Str("service", service).
		Msgf("Cannot start %s", service)

	// Outputs: {"level":"fatal","time":1199811905,"error":"A repo man spends his life getting into tense situations","service":"myservice","message":"Cannot start myservice"}
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Level"></a>
## func [Level](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L27>)

```go
func Level(level zerolog.Level) zerolog.Logger
```

Level creates a child logger with the minimum accepted level set to level.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of using the Level function to set the log level

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()

	// you have to assign the result of Level() to a new Logger and can't inline the level calls
	// because they need a *Logger receiver
	leveled := log.Level(zerolog.ErrorLevel)
	leveled.Info().Msg("hello world")
	leveled.Error().Msg("I said HELLO")
}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"error","time":1199811905,"message":"I said HELLO"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Log"></a>
## func [Log](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L111>)

```go
func Log() *zerolog.Event
```

Log starts a new message with no level. Setting zerolog.GlobalLevel to zerolog.Disabled will still disable events produced by this method.

You must call Msg on the returned event in order to send the event.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of a log with no particular "level"

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()
	log.Log().Msg("hello world")

}
```

#### Output

```
{"time":1199811905,"message":"hello world"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Output"></a>
## func [Output](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L17>)

```go
func Output(w io.Writer) zerolog.Logger
```

Output duplicates the global logger and sets w as its output.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of using the Output function in the log package to change the output destination

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()

	out := &bytes.Buffer{}
	tee := log.Output(out)
	tee.Info().Msg("hello world")
	written := out.Len()

	log.Info().Int("bytes", written).Msg("wrote")
}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"info","bytes":59,"time":1199811905,"message":"wrote"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Panic"></a>
## func [Panic](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L96>)

```go
func Panic() *zerolog.Event
```

Panic starts a new message with panic level. The message is also sent to the panic function.

You must call Msg on the returned event in order to send the event.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of a log at a particular "level" \(in this case, "panic"\)

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()

	log.Panic().Msg("Cannot start")
	// Outputs: {"level":"panic","time":1199811905,"message":"Cannot start"} then panics
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Print"></a>
## func [Print](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L117>)

```go
func Print(v ...interface{})
```

Print sends a log event using debug level and no extra field. Arguments are handled in the manner of fmt.Print.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Simple logging example using the Print function in the log package Note that both Print and Printf are at the debug log level by default

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()

	log.Print("hello world")
}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"debug","time":1199811905,"message":"hello world"}
```

</p>
</details>

```go
func Printf(format string, v ...interface{})
```

Printf sends a log event using debug level and no extra field. Arguments are handled in the manner of fmt.Printf.

<a name="Sample"></a>
## func [Sample](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L32>)

```go
func Sample(s zerolog.Sampler) zerolog.Logger
```

Sample returns a logger with the s sampler.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of using the Sample function in the log package to set a sampler

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()

	sampled := log.Sample(&zerolog.BasicSampler{N: 2})
	sampled.Info().Msg("hello world")
	sampled.Info().Msg("I said, hello world")
	sampled.Info().Msg("Can you here me now world")
}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"info","time":1199811905,"message":"hello world"}
{"level":"info","time":1199811905,"message":"Can you here me now world"}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="With"></a>
## func [With](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/log/log.go#L22>)

```go
func With() zerolog.Context
```

With creates a child logger with the field added to its context.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of using the With function to add context fields

```go
package main

import (
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/rs/zerolog"
	"github.com/rs/zerolog/log"
)

// setup would normally be an init() function, however, there seems
// to be something awry with the testing framework when we set the
// global Logger from an init()
func setup() {

	zerolog.TimeFieldFormat = ""

	zerolog.TimestampFunc = func() time.Time {
		return time.Date(2008, 1, 8, 17, 5, 05, 0, time.UTC)
	}
	log.Logger = zerolog.New(os.Stdout).With().Timestamp().Logger()
}

func main() {
	setup()

	// you have to assign the result of With() to a new Logger and can't inline the level calls
	// because they need a *Logger receiver
	augmented := log.With().Str("service", "myservice").Logger()
	augmented.Info().Msg("hello world")
}
```

#### Output

```
{"level":"info","service":"myservice","time":1199811905,"message":"hello world"}
```

</p>
</details>


WithLevel starts a new message with level.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Example of using the WithLevel function to set the log level

```go
package main
```

# cbor

```go
import "github.com/rs/zerolog/internal/cbor"
```

Package cbor provides primitives for storing different data in the CBOR \(binary\) format. CBOR is defined in RFC7049.

## Variables

<a name="IntegerTimeFieldFormat"></a>IntegerTimeFieldFormat indicates the format of timestamp decoded from an integer \(time in seconds\).

```go
var IntegerTimeFieldFormat = time.RFC3339
```

<a name="JSONMarshalFunc"></a>JSONMarshalFunc is used to marshal interface to JSON encoded byte slice. Making it package level instead of embedded in Encoder brings some extra efforts at importing, but avoids value copy when the functions of Encoder being invoked. DO REMEMBER to set this variable at importing, or you might get a nil pointer dereference panic at runtime.

```go
var JSONMarshalFunc func(v interface{}) ([]byte, error)
```

<a name="NanoTimeFieldFormat"></a>NanoTimeFieldFormat indicates the format of timestamp decoded from a float value \(time in seconds and nanoseconds\).

```go
var NanoTimeFieldFormat = time.RFC3339Nano
```

<a name="AppendEmbeddedCBOR"></a>

```go
func AppendEmbeddedCBOR(dst, s []byte) []byte
```

## func [AppendEmbeddedJSON](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/internal/cbor/string.go#L75>)

```go
func AppendEmbeddedJSON(dst, s []byte) []byte
```

AppendEmbeddedJSON adds a tag and embeds input JSON as such.

<a name="Cbor2JsonManyObjects"></a>
## func [Cbor2JsonManyObjects](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/internal/cbor/decode_stream.go#L594>)

```go
func Cbor2JsonManyObjects(src io.Reader, dst io.Writer) (err error)
```

Cbor2JsonManyObjects decodes all the CBOR Objects read from src reader. It keeps on decoding until reader returns EOF \(error when reading\). Decoded string is written to the dst. At the end of every CBOR Object newline is written to the output stream.

Returns error \(if any\) that was encountered during decode. The child functions will generate a panic when error is encountered and this function will recover non\-runtime Errors and return the reason as error.

<a name="DecodeIfBinaryToBytes"></a>
## func [DecodeIfBinaryToBytes](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/internal/cbor/decode_stream.go#L647>)

```go
func DecodeIfBinaryToBytes(in []byte) []byte
```

DecodeIfBinaryToBytes checks if the input is a binary format, if so, it will decode all Objects and return the decoded string as byte array.

<a name="DecodeIfBinaryToString"></a>
## func [DecodeIfBinaryToString](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/internal/cbor/decode_stream.go#L625>)

```go
func DecodeIfBinaryToString(in []byte) string
```

DecodeIfBinaryToString converts a binary formatted log msg to a JSON formatted String Log message \- suitable for printing to Console/Syslog.

<a name="DecodeObjectToStr"></a>
## func [DecodeObjectToStr](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/internal/cbor/decode_stream.go#L636>)

```go
func DecodeObjectToStr(in []byte) string
```

DecodeObjectToStr checks if the input is a binary format, if so, it will decode a single Object and return the decoded string.

## Variables

<a name="JSONMarshalFunc"></a>JSONMarshalFunc is used to marshal interface to JSON encoded byte slice. Making it package level instead of embedded in Encoder brings some extra efforts at importing, but avoids value copy when the functions of Encoder being invoked. DO REMEMBER to set this variable at importing, or you might get a nil pointer dereference panic at runtime.

```go
var JSONMarshalFunc func(v interface{}) ([]byte, error)
```

<a name="Encoder.AppendString"></a>
### func \(Encoder\) [AppendString](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/internal/json/string.go#L44>)

```go
func (Encoder) AppendString(dst []byte, s string) []byte
```

AppendString encodes the input string to json and appends the encoded string to the input byte slice.

The operation loops though each byte in the string looking for characters that need json or utf8 encoding. If the string does not need encoding, then the string is appended in its entirety to the byte slice. If we encounter a byte that does need encoding, switch up the operation and perform a byte\-by\-byte read\-encode\-append.

<a name="AlertFunc"></a>
## type [AlertFunc](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L19>)

AlertFunc type is an adapter to allow the use of ordinary functions as Alert handlers.

```go
type AlertFunc func(missed int)
```

<a name="AlertFunc.Alert"></a>
### func \(AlertFunc\) [Alert](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L22>)

```go
func (f AlertFunc) Alert(missed int)
```

Alert calls f\(missed\)

<a name="Alerter"></a>
## type [Alerter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L13-L15>)

Alerter is used to report how many values were overwritten since the last write.

```go
type Alerter interface {
    Alert(missed int)
}
```

<a name="Diode"></a>
## type [Diode](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/poller.go#L9-L12>)

Diode is any implementation of a diode.

```go
type Diode interface {
    Set(GenericDataType)
    TryNext() (GenericDataType, bool)
}
```

<a name="GenericDataType"></a>
## type [GenericDataType](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L9>)

GenericDataType is the data type the diodes operate on.

```go
type GenericDataType unsafe.Pointer
```

<a name="ManyToOne"></a>
## type [ManyToOne](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/many_to_one.go#L11-L16>)

ManyToOne diode is optimal for many writers \(go\-routines B\-n\) and a single reader \(go\-routine A\). It is not thread safe for multiple readers.

```go
type ManyToOne struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewManyToOne"></a>
### func [NewManyToOne](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/many_to_one.go#L23>)

```go
func NewManyToOne(size int, alerter Alerter) *ManyToOne
```

NewManyToOne creates a new diode \(ring buffer\). The ManyToOne diode is optimized for many writers \(on go\-routines B\-n\) and a single reader \(on go\-routine A\). The alerter is invoked on the read's go\-routine. It is called when it notices that the writer go\-routine has passed it and wrote over data. A nil can be used to ignore alerts.

<a name="ManyToOne.Set"></a>
### func \(\*ManyToOne\) [Set](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/many_to_one.go#L41>)

```go
func (d *ManyToOne) Set(data GenericDataType)
```

Set sets the data in the next slot of the ring buffer.

<a name="ManyToOne.TryNext"></a>
### func \(\*ManyToOne\) [TryNext](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/many_to_one.go#L70>)

```go
func (d *ManyToOne) TryNext() (data GenericDataType, ok bool)
```

TryNext will attempt to read from the next slot of the ring buffer. If there is no data available, it will return \(nil, false\).

<a name="OneToOne"></a>
## type [OneToOne](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L33-L38>)

OneToOne diode is meant to be used by a single reader and a single writer. It is not thread safe if used otherwise.

```go
type OneToOne struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewOneToOne"></a>
### func [NewOneToOne](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L44>)

```go
func NewOneToOne(size int, alerter Alerter) *OneToOne
```

NewOneToOne creates a new diode is meant to be used by a single reader and a single writer. The alerter is invoked on the read's go\-routine. It is called when it notices that the writer go\-routine has passed it and wrote over data. A nil can be used to ignore alerts.

<a name="OneToOne.Set"></a>
### func \(\*OneToOne\) [Set](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L56>)

```go
func (d *OneToOne) Set(data GenericDataType)
```

Set sets the data in the next slot of the ring buffer.

<a name="OneToOne.TryNext"></a>
### func \(\*OneToOne\) [TryNext](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/one_to_one.go#L70>)

```go
func (d *OneToOne) TryNext() (data GenericDataType, ok bool)
```

TryNext will attempt to read from the next slot of the ring buffer. If there is no data available, it will return \(nil, false\).

<a name="Poller"></a>
## type [Poller](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/poller.go#L15-L19>)

Poller will poll a diode until a value is available.

```go
type Poller struct {
    Diode
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewPoller"></a>
### func [NewPoller](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/poller.go#L42>)

```go
func NewPoller(d Diode, opts ...PollerConfigOption) *Poller
```

NewPoller returns a new Poller that wraps the given diode.

<a name="Poller.Next"></a>
### func \(\*Poller\) [Next](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/poller.go#L58>)

```go
func (p *Poller) Next() GenericDataType
```

Next polls the diode until data is available or until the context is done. If the context is done, then nil will be returned.

<a name="PollerConfigOption"></a>
## type [PollerConfigOption](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/poller.go#L22>)

PollerConfigOption can be used to setup the poller.

```go
type PollerConfigOption func(*Poller)
```

<a name="WithPollingContext"></a>
### func [WithPollingContext](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/poller.go#L35>)

```go
func WithPollingContext(ctx context.Context) PollerConfigOption
```

WithPollingContext sets the context to cancel any retrieval \(Next\(\)\). It will not change any results for adding data \(Set\(\)\). Default is context.Background\(\).

<a name="WithPollingInterval"></a>
### func [WithPollingInterval](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/poller.go#L26>)

```go
func WithPollingInterval(interval time.Duration) PollerConfigOption
```

WithPollingInterval sets the interval at which the diode is queried for new data. The default is 10ms.

<a name="Waiter"></a>
## type [Waiter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/waiter.go#L10-L15>)

Waiter will use a conditional mutex to alert the reader to when data is available.

```go
type Waiter struct {
    Diode
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewWaiter"></a>
### func [NewWaiter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/waiter.go#L30>)

```go
func NewWaiter(d Diode, opts ...WaiterConfigOption) *Waiter
```

NewWaiter returns a new Waiter that wraps the given diode.

<a name="Waiter.Next"></a>
### func \(\*Waiter\) [Next](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/waiter.go#L63>)

```go
func (w *Waiter) Next() GenericDataType
```

Next returns the next data point on the wrapped diode. If there is not any new data, it will Wait for set to be called or the context to be done. If the context is done, then nil will be returned.

<a name="Waiter.Set"></a>
### func \(\*Waiter\) [Set](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/waiter.go#L55>)

```go
func (w *Waiter) Set(data GenericDataType)
```

Set invokes the wrapped diode's Set with the given data and uses Broadcast to wake up any readers.

<a name="WaiterConfigOption"></a>
## type [WaiterConfigOption](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/waiter.go#L18>)

WaiterConfigOption can be used to setup the waiter.

```go
type WaiterConfigOption func(*Waiter)
```

<a name="WithWaiterContext"></a>
### func [WithWaiterContext](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/diode/internal/diodes/waiter.go#L23>)

```go
func WithWaiterContext(ctx context.Context) WaiterConfigOption
```

WithWaiterContext sets the context to cancel any retrieval \(Next\(\)\). It will not change any results for adding data \(Set\(\)\). Default is context.Background\(\).

# mutil

```go
import "github.com/rs/zerolog/hlog/internal/mutil"
```

Package mutil contains various functions that are helpful when writing http middleware.

It has been vendored from Goji v1.0, with the exception of the code for Go 1.8: https://github.com/zenazn/goji/

## Index

- [type WriterProxy](<#WriterProxy>)
  - [func WrapWriter\(w http.ResponseWriter\) WriterProxy](<#WrapWriter>)


<a name="WriterProxy"></a>
## type [WriterProxy](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/hlog/internal/mutil/writer_proxy.go#L12-L28>)

WriterProxy is a proxy around an http.ResponseWriter that allows you to hook into various parts of the response process.

```go
type WriterProxy interface {
    http.ResponseWriter
    // Status returns the HTTP status of the request, or 0 if one has not
    // yet been sent.
    Status() int
    // BytesWritten returns the total number of bytes sent to the client.
    BytesWritten() int
    // Tee causes the response body to be written to the given io.Writer in
    // addition to proxying the writes through. Only one io.Writer can be
    // tee'd to at once: setting a second one will overwrite the first.
    // Writes will be sent to the proxy before being written to this
    // io.Writer. It is illegal for the tee'd writer to be modified
    // concurrently with writes.
    Tee(io.Writer)
    // Unwrap returns the original proxied target.
    Unwrap() http.ResponseWriter
}
```

<a name="WrapWriter"></a>
### func [WrapWriter](<https://github.com/rs/zerolog/blob/master/hlog/internal/mutil/writer_proxy.go#L32>)

```go
func WrapWriter(w http.ResponseWriter) WriterProxy
```

WrapWriter wraps an http.ResponseWriter, returning a proxy that allows you to hook into various parts of the response process.
